methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Fuse Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M+

data.narrative:
Gauss Fusion is a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Milena Roveda that has raised €9M to date. The relevant peer universe comprises 8 private companies operating in nuclear energy alongside 3 public comparables (OKLO, NNE, SMR). Top private peers include Fuse Energy, Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power.

The peer-median method is not applicable to this analysis. The nuclear energy sector, while represented by 8 private companies, lacks sufficient comparable-stage data points to support a robust median valuation. Only 1 same-stage peer and 1 adjacent-stage peer were identified within acceptable valuation bands, falling short of the minimum 3 datapoints required for statistical reliability. An all-sector median would introduce unacceptable distortion given the narrow applicability to Gauss Fusion's specific development phase and technology profile.

Without a triangulated valuation result derived from this dataset, a point estimate cannot be provided. The analysis underscores a structural limitation: Series A nuclear energy companies are sparse in both public and private markets, and those that are disclosed operate across heterogeneous technology platforms and capital structures. A credible valuation would require either (1) expanding the stage filter to capture adjacent-stage transactions with disclosed terms, (2) applying cost-of-capital or revenue-multiple methods using company-specific financial projections, or (3) supplementing with recent transaction data from the identified peer group. Key uncertainties include technology readiness assumptions, regulatory pathway clarity, and capital-intensity through commercialization.
